dsl_rule.translation_notes: Entry: Extreme single-day decline (3.5% threshold) detected via negative returns and elevated volatility; RSI < 30 confirms oversold/panic condition. Exit: 50% reversion of initial decline or volatility normalization. Psychological confirmation of emotional-driven move cannot be coded mechanically—assumed when extreme vol + RSI extreme occur together. Position sizing (add 5% on move) and reduction schedule (10% per week over 2 weeks) are execution parameters not encoded in rule conditions. Max exposure capped at 75% via position management layer.

backtest_rule.reason: Entry: Extreme single-day decline (3.5% threshold) detected via negative returns and elevated volatility; RSI < 30 confirms oversold/panic condition. Exit: 50% reversion of initial decline or volatility normalization. Psychological confirmation of emotional-driven move cannot be coded mechanically—assumed when extreme vol + RSI extreme occur together. Position sizing (add 5% on move) and reduction schedule (10% per week over 2 weeks) are execution parameters not encoded in rule conditions. Max exposure capped at 75% via position management layer.

source_rule_text: A trader should increase exposure to contrarian positions during extreme market moves, maintaining emotional distance from the panic/euphoria driving the move, and then systematically reduce exposure as the magnitude of decline continues.
